english: gri-só (gri so), gri-dṅó (gri dngo), gri-k̇á (gri kha) the edge of a knife; gri-lám (gri lam) lit. 'the path of the knife', the cut, incision; gri-gúg (gri gug) Pth. a short, crooked sabre or sword, falchion, cimeter; gri-ṡá (gri sha) flesh of a man that has been killed with a sword, (used in sorcery). — 2. Lt.: dar-mai gri (dar ma'i gri)?
Mil., prob. = sgrín-po (sgrin po) skilful, clever.

1. shade, grib-kyi ṕu (grib kyi phu) Glr. the shady part of a valley on the north side of a mountain range, cf. sribs (sribs); grib-pýogs (grib phyogs) the side not exposed to the sun, north side, col., grib-lhags (grib lhags) the coolness of the shade, the cool shade Sch.; grib-ma (grib ma) *ḍi-mág* (*di mag*) W. shadow (cast by an object); dei grib-ma gáṅ-la póg-pa (de'i grib ma gang la phog pa) on whom his shadow falls; grib-tsód (grib tshod) a dial Cs. — 2. spot, filth, defilement, contamination, mostly in a religious sense: grib yoṅ (grib yong) pollution arises; ro-grib (ro grib) defilement by a corpse; grib-sél (grib sel) name of a Buddha; grib-(kyis) nón-gyi ydon (grib kyis non gyi gdon) a demon that defiles and poisons the food, a harpy; *kó-la ḍib póg soṅ* (*kho la dib phog song*) W. C. he is crack-brained, not in his right mind; *ḍib-c̀an* (*dib can*) stubborn, refractory, whether from stupidity, or from ill-will.
Med.? (Lex. quadrangular, regular, harmonious) Schr.: intelligent, clever.
(cf. gríl-ba (gril ba)) a roll, ṡog-gril (shog gril) rolled paper, a paper-roll; gos-gril (gos gril) a garment folded up Cs.; gril-k̇a byéd-pa (gril kha byed pa) to make up a parcel Sch.

1. boat, ferry, ship, vessel, also a hide blown up with air, used for crossing rivers = *ko-ḍhú* (*ko dhu*) C.; gru-ṡán (gru shan) id.; gru-ṡán-pa (gru shan pa) ferry-man; grú-la żón-pa (gru la zhon pa) to go on a ferry. Comp, grú-k̇a (gru kha), gru-ṡán-k̇a (gru shan kha), grú-btaṅ-sa (gru btang sa) C. starting- or landing-place of a ferry. — gru-glá (gru gla), gru-btsás (gru btsas) fare, passage-money, a boat-man's fee. — grú-pa (gru pa) ferry-man. — gru-dpón (gru dpon) ship-master, master of a vessel. — grú-bo (gru bo), gen. gru-yziṅs (gru gzings), ship. — gru-°dźín (gru 'dzin) ancient name of Tatta, at the mouth of the Indus, ancestral seat of the Shakya race, whence the name is transferred to the residence of the Dalai Lama in Lhasa, v. Köpp. II, 342. — 2. (Cs. grú-ma (gru ma)) angle, corner, convex or concave, also edge, border, brim; gru-ysúm (gru gsum), gru-bżí (gru bzhi) etc. triangle, quadrangle; gru-ysúm-pa (gru gsum pa) triangular; dkyil-k̇ór gru-bżí-pa żig brí-ba (dkyil 'khor gru bzhi pa zhig bri ba) to draw a quadrangular figure, a square; °dom-gáṅ gru-bżí ('dom gang gru bzhi), a surface six feet square; dbyibs gru-bżír yod (dbyibs gru bzhir yod) S.g.; *ḍu-nar-c̀an* (*du nar can*) W. rhomboidal; gru-yon (gru yon), Cs. gru-gyél (gru gyel), oblique angled; gru-drán (gru drang) right-angled Cs.; gru-k̇úṅ (gru khung) v. mto-gón (mtho gon). — yúl-gru (yul gru) place, village, town, country. — 3. lustre, of precious stones, gru-dmár (gru dmar) a reddish lustre Mil.nt.
1. clew, hank. — 2. n. of a country.

elbow, grú-mor ka-tvám-ka bzúṅ-ba (gru mor ka tvam ka bzung ba) holding a trident in his arm Pth.; dé-la grú-moi ṕul-rdég c̀ig byás-nas (de la gru mo'i phul rdeg cig byas nas) pushing him with his elbow Mil.; gru-súg byéd-pa (gru sug byed pa) id.; grú-moi k̇ug (gru mo'i khug), the hollow of the elbow-joint Glr.
or gru-ṡă (gru sha), n. of a country Pth.

to break into small pieces, to crumble, to bruise Dzl.; grúg-pai °bras (grug pa'i 'bras) bruised rice Schr.; rús-pa c̀ag-grúgs (rus pa chag grugs) fracture of a bone Med.; grúgs-bu (grugs bu) something broken.
fem. grúṅ-mo (grung mo) 1. wise, prudent Mil.; also: grúṅs-pa lags (grungs pa lags) very learned Sir! Thgr. — 2. meek, mild, gentle Cs.
Ld. all, *ḍub ṡi soṅ* (*dub shi song*) all are dead; *ḍub zas soṅ* (*dub zas song*) it has all been eaten up, (v. the next word).

pf. of °grúb-pa ('grub pa) 1. made ready, complete; perfect; (ma grúb-pa (ma grub pa) also: not existing); grúb-pai raṅ-byóṅ spyan-ras-yzígs (grub pa'i rang byong spyan ras gzigs) Glr. the perfect, by himself originated, Awalokiteswara = lhún-gyis grúb-pa (lhun gyis grub pa); don tams-c̀ád grub-pa (don thams cad grub pa), don-grub (don grub), 'the fulfilment of every wish' n. of Buddha, also of a spell or
